overview: Dimir Control in Pauper focuses on surviving the early game with efficient removal and board wipes, then stabilizing with resilient creatures and card advantage. By leveraging signature cards like Snuff Out and Lorien Revealed, the deck clears opposing boards while maintaining its own resources. Tolarian Terror provides a durable, value-generating finisher that pressures opponents over time.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through card advantage and board control
Pressuring with resilient threats like Tolarian Terror
Winning via combat damage after clearing the board with Lorien Revealed

keep_criteria: Hands with early interaction like Snuff Out
Mana bases that support two-color casting by turn three
Combinations of removal and a late-game threat
mulligan_criteria: Mana screws with three or more lands
Hands lacking any removal or board impact
Overloaded hands with only late-game cards

early_game: Play lands and hold up mana for Snuff Out
Trade early threats efficiently to preserve life total
Prioritize drawing into more removal and board wipes
mid_game: Cast Lorien Revealed to reset the board when ahead or at parity
Deploy Tolarian Terror to establish a persistent threat
Use card draw and filtering to find answers to opposing strategies
late_game: Maintain board presence with Tolarian Terror and other resilient creatures
Answer opposing finishers with remaining removal
Close out the game by trading up or attacking with a larger board

key_cards.name: Tolarian Terror
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have card advantage or board control; its +1/+1 counter generation and flying make it difficult to remove without multiple answers.

key_cards.name: Snuff Out
key_cards.notes: Use to kill low-toughness threats or finish off damaged creatures; prioritize targets that would otherwise pressure your life total.

key_cards.name: Lorien Revealed
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have few or no permanents to clear the board; follow up with Tolarian Terror or other threats to regain initiative.

sequencing_tips: Hold up black mana for Snuff Out during opponent's turn to catch fast threats
Wait to cast Lorien Revealed until after opponents play their key threats or tap into combat
Cast Tolarian Terror on your turn when you can protect it or trade efficiently

matchups.advice: Prioritize early removal and life total preservation; use Lorien Revealed to reset aggressive boards.
Race to board wipes and value engines; avoid trading removal into unremovable threats.
Establish board presence early with Tolarian Terror; force opponents to use removal before casting board wipes.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Dimir Control archetype in Pauper format

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Standard control sequencing principles applied to Pauper Dimir
